operating_state: AMD delivered 14% revenue growth to $25.8 billion in 2024, driven by Data Center segment surging 94% from strong AMD Instinct GPU and EPYC CPU sales to hyperscale customers, and Client segment rising 52% from higher Ryzen processor demand. Gaming revenue declined 58% due to lower semi-custom product sales, while Embedded revenue fell 33% as customers normalized inventory levels. International sales represented 66% of net revenue with nearly all product assembly and final testing occurring at third-party facilities in Asia.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 49% from 46% in the prior year, primarily driven by favorable revenue mix shift toward higher-margin Data Center and Client segments, partially offset by lower Embedded revenue contribution. Operating expenses increased with R&D rising 10% due to higher headcount supporting AI strategy, and marketing expenses growing 18% from expanded go-to-market activities. Manufacturing costs remain subject to third-party foundry pricing with TSMC handling advanced nodes while GLOBALFOUNDRIES manages larger nodes.
material_events: AMD completed acquisition of Silo AI in August 2024 and agreed to acquire ZT Systems for approximately $3.4 billion in cash and stock to accelerate AI infrastructure capabilities, with closing expected in first half 2025 subject to regulatory approvals. The company repurchased $862 million of shares during 2024 and repaid $750 million of Senior Notes in June, maintaining $4.7 billion available under stock repurchase program.
macro_exposures: Export control restrictions implemented in October 2023 prevent shipment of certain AMD Instinct integrated circuits and Versal FPGAs to China and D5 country customers without licenses, requiring 25-day NAC notifications for specific products. The company faces exposure to geopolitical tensions between China and Taiwan that could disrupt foundry operations, ongoing regional conflicts, and potential escalation of trade restrictions.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued focus on AI strategy execution with annual cadence of leadership AI solutions through accelerated AMD Instinct roadmap and building AI capabilities into all product lines including Data Center accelerators and Client processors. The company intends to seek a strategic partner to acquire ZT Systems' manufacturing business following the acquisition closing and believes current liquidity including $5.1 billion cash position will fund operations and growth investments.
uncertainty_profile: ZT Systems acquisition faces regulatory approval risks with $300 million termination fee if approval-related conditions cause deal failure, plus potential additional payments based on performance conditions. AMD's joint ventures with Chinese entity THATIC remain on the Entity List since June 2019, limiting operational flexibility, while dependence on TSMC for advanced node production creates supply chain vulnerabilities. The company faces potential asset impairment risks for goodwill and intangible assets from recent acquisitions.
